title: [CI:DOCS] docs: make CNI removal explicit
body:
The docs were rather vague about the deprecation of CNI, make it clear that we are going to remove it with 5.0 as we decided to do that in our planning.

Also while looking at the podman network create docs I noticed --ipam-driver dhcp was still documented as not supported with netavark so I fixed that as well.

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Deprecation notice: CNI support will be removed in the next major version 5.0.
```

LGTM. We should do a blogpost on this as well.

> LGTM. We should do a blogpost on this as well.

Agreed, I can write one later.

@Luap99 and @mheon, I may have missed the "pasta" deprecation.  When was that deprecated?  We just added notes to RHEL 9.3 about it and were planning a user story for 9.4.   Does Gabi need to adjust documents for RHEL?

> @Luap99 and @mheon, I may have missed the "pasta" deprecation. When was that deprecated? We just added notes to RHEL 9.3 about it and were planning a user story for 9.4. Does Gabi need to adjust documents for RHEL?

Please read the sentence carefully again, we are not deprecating `pasta`, we are depreciating networks with the name `pasta` as they conflict with the actual `pasta` mode. If the sentence is misunderstood then please suggest on how to make this clearer.
Oh man @Luap99 , I don't know why, but the word "name" never registered when I read that sentence.  Let me go tweak that sentence a bit, and thanks for the clarification.
